question: The three major political parties of the First Republic can be said to have had __
solution: The major political parties of the First Republic were largely based on regional and ethnic divisions.

question: One of the problems of the Commonwealth of Nations is lack of __
solution: The Commonwealth lacks the power to enforce its decisions.

question: A major objective of Public Complaints Commission is __

option.a: creating fair opportunities for all government employees
option.c: addressing grievances of individuals and groups

solution: The Public Complaints Commission deals with individual and group grievances.

question: Activities of pressure groups that influence governmental decisions are hampered by __
solution: Economic conditions can significantly impact the activities of pressure groups.

question: Which of the following is a major feature of democracy?
solution: Democracy is based on the consent of the governed.

question: Nigeria became a republic with the __
solution: Nigeria became a republic under the 1963 Constitution (though the 1960 Constitution led to independence).

question: The Constitutional forms with two levels of Government is _________
solution: Confederation is the Constitutional form with the levels of Government, the Central and the Regional

question: The essence of ECOWAS is to________

option.b: foster interstate economic and political co-operation

solution: The Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS) is a regional organisation of 15 West African countries established on 28 May 1975. Its main goal is the promotion of the economic integration among its members. Indeed, ECOWASis one the five regional pillars of the African Economic Community (AEC).

question: The granting of internal authority to the regions came under which constitution?
solution: This constitution was the only constitution that granted Internal authority

question: The legal right to exercise power in a state is known as______
solution: Authority is the legitimate power or right to give orders, make decisions, and enforce obedience.

question: Which of the following is a legal source of political authority?
solution: In a democracy, political authority comes from the people through elections.

question: Which organ of government is vested with the responsibility of initiating bills and recommending them to the legislature for consideration?
solution: The executive arm initiates bills and presents them to the legislature for consideration.

question: In a republic parliamentary system of government, the ceremonial Head of State is the
solution: In a parliamentary system, the President is the ceremonial Head of State.

question: Which of the following is the lowest in the hierarchy of feudal system?
solution: Serfs were the lowest class in the feudal system.

question: A constitution is the
solution: A constitution is the supreme set of laws guiding a nation.

question: The second ballot is based on the principle that a successful candidate must obtain
solution:
Before the Second Ballot System is introduce in an election is when none of the candidates wins the election by absolute majority in a general election, another election would be conducted. In this final election, only the candidates with the highest votes are allowed to contest. 

So the second ballot is based on the principle that a successful candidate must obtain Absolute Majority

Absolute Majority System is a system of voting in which a candidate must acquire more than half of the total vote cast before he can be declared winner.

question: Deconcentration of power means the_______

option.a: sharing of powers among tiers of government
option.b: creation of semi autonomous units to govern other parts of a state
option.c: process of setting strategies or policies for the subsidiary territories in the state by putting recognized personnel in place
option.d: act of delegating powers and authority to component units that exist in a state

solution:
Deconcentration involves the passing down of selected administrative functions to lower levels or units within government agencies or departments with the centre remaining in control

Like the series of political office arrangements and portfolios in the Hausa/Fulani Empire i.e the Galadima, Waziri and the rest, deconcentration of power is obtainable unlike in devolution which Options B and D implicitly cover. Option A refers to the Decentralization of powers.

question: A cabinet reshuffle takes place......

option.a: only when a motion of no confidence is passed
option.c: when prorogation and collective responsibility have their way
option.d: when the shadow cabinet fails in its effectiveness

solution: A cabinet reshuffle takes place when dissolution is held. In parliamentary systems, a cabinet reshuffle may occur when there is a general election and the government is dissolved, leading to a need for reorganizing the cabinet with new ministers or changing existing ones. The membership of the Cabinet will be substituted or reshuffled, especially when there is the dissolution of the cabinet in its entirety in a Parliamentary/ Westminster system.

question: The non-stratified society in the pre-colonial era was that of the ........ where decisions were made________
solution: The Igbo precolonial was seen as ''headless'' and decisions were made based on popular consultations and acceptability.

question: Twelve States creation is a known achievement of the ... Regime
solution: Yakubu Gowon created 12 states for Nigeria in 1967.

question: The Brussel conference in 1889/90 was based on______

option.a: putting an end to slave trade
option.c: furthering the decision on colonialism as previously discussed in the Berlin conference
option.d: deciding on how to penetrate Africa for economic adventurism

solution: The Brussel Conference unlike the Berlin Conference was targeted at the reconsideration of the unjust virtues and vices occasioned by colonialism, thereby specifically seeking to eradicate slave trade and related activities.

question: In the all-Nigerian constitutional conference of 1950_______

option.a: all official members of the legislative council participated
option.b: it was resolved that the Nigerians federation be organized on ethnic basis
option.c: it was concluded that a quasi-federal structure should be developed for colonial Nigeria
option.d: all unofficial members of the legislative council participated

solution: Recognizing and acknowledging the relative importance of direct participation and contribution by people in their own affairs, the All-Nigerian Constitutional Conference marked an unprecendented attendance of all previously relegated unofficial members of the local legislature.

question: The most recent target plans and agenda of the United Nations are known as the______
solution: The 17 United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are the most recent aspirational global agenda for the sake of environmental sustainability, strong governance institutions and partnerships among related others.

question: The federal judge under the 1963 constitution could be removed by the______
solution: 2/3 majority support or vote of the National Assembly members was enough under the 1963 Constitution to remove a federal judge.

question: Amendment of the U. N. O. charter is the duty of the ........
solution: The UN General Assembly has the duty of amending relevant provisions of the UN Charter.

question: The delineation of electoral constituencies for reasons of political advantage is termed___________
solution: Gerrymandering is the process of creating political constituencies in order to favour the electoral scores of a political party.

question: A predominant feature of the Precolonial governance system was that_________

option.a: the British Indirect rule system penetrated it largely
option.b: the governance structures were largely undefined

solution: Throughout the precolonial era in Nigeria, the native laws of the people governed them. They were largely unwritten and enforced by distinct units set in place for those purposes.

question: Class stratification is a peculiar feature of___________
solution: In a capitalist system, there is a clear differentiation gap between the ruling class and the ruled or the proletarates.

question: The judicial organ of government is the body which _________
solution: The judicial organ of government performs various functions which include; punishment of offenders, settlement of disputes, safeguarding rights, protection of constitution etc. However, in summary, it is the body that interprets the law of the state.

question: In a democracy, sovereignty is in _________
solution: Democracy is concentrated on the people, the people's ability to exercise power directly or through a representative.

question: A bill that applies to the whole population and is intended to promote the general welfare is called _________
solution: Public bills are proposed laws that affect the general public and are intended to address broad issues or policy areas, rather than being tailored to specific individuals or organizations.

question: The theory of separation of powers was first time clearly formulated by __________
solution:
The theory of separation of powers was first clearly formulated by Baron de Montesquieu in his book The Spirit of the Laws (1748).

Montesquieu argued that government power should be divided among three branches—the legislative, executive, and judiciary—to prevent abuse of power.
This theory influenced modern democratic governance, particularly the U.S. Constitution and many other constitutional governments worldwide.

question: A system of government in which power derives from total control of the instruments of force is called ___________
solution: Facism is a form of government headed by a dictator, in which government has a total control over all activities in the state.

question: The Fundamental Objectives and Directive Principles of State Policy in the 1979 constitution do not include _________

option.c: concentration of wealth and provision of maximum welfare

solution:
The Fundamental Objectives and Directive Principles of State Policy in the 1979 Nigerian Constitution were designed to guide government policies and promote national development. These principles focused on democracy, social justice, federal character, national integration, and equal opportunities for all citizens.

The constitution aimed to reduce the concentration of wealth in the hands of a few and instead promote fair distribution of resources and social welfare.
Option C is incorrect because "concentration of wealth" contradicts the principles of the constitution.

question: Africans were first elected to the legislative council in British West Africa in _________
solution: In 1923, Nigerians were first elected to the legislative council in British West Africa.

question: A set of internalized norms which guides political action is called ________
solution: Laws are formal rules established by a governing authority that are expected to be followed by members of society. They guide behaviour and political action by providing a framework for acceptable conduct and legal processes.

question: In the First Republic, Nigeria was very reluctant to have meaningful interaction with ________
solution:
During Nigeria’s First Republic (1963–1966), the country followed a pro-Western foreign policy, maintaining close ties with Western nations like the United States, the United Kingdom, and other Commonwealth countries.

Nigeria was reluctant to engage meaningfully with the Soviet Union due to the Cold War tensions and fear of communist influence in Africa.
The government, led by Prime Minister Sir Abubakar Tafawa Balewa, aligned more with capitalist democracies rather than the socialist bloc.

question: Which of the following was the last to win independence from colonial rule ?
solution:
Angola was the last among the options to gain independence from colonial rule.

Côte d'Ivoire – Gained independence from France in 1960.
Algeria – Gained independence from France in 1962 after a long and bloody war.
Tanzania – (Formerly Tanganyika) Gained independence from Britain in 1961, later uniting with Zanzibar in 1964.
Angola – Gained independence from Portugal in 1975, making it the last among these options.

question: The charter of the United Nations was drawn up in ________
solution: The charter of the United Nations was signed on 26th June 1945 in San Francisco

question: During the Civil War, the major power that expressed moral support for Biafra's self- determination was ......
solution:
During the Civil War, the major power that expressed moral support for Biafra's self-determination was France.

France gave both financial and material assistance to Biafra.

question: When Nigeria achieved independence in 1960, the head of state was the _____
solution: When Nigeria gained independence from the United Kingdom on 1 October 1960, it originally had a parliamentary constitutional monarchy with Queen Elizabeth II as the head of state.

question: After 1945, the demand of African nationalists changed from reform to independence because .....

option.a: The Second World War enhanced colonial rule
option.d: The Second World War boosted their morale

solution: After 1945, the demand of African nationalists changed from reform to independence because the second world war boosted their morale. The war exposed the myth surrounding white supremacy.

question: One argument against a multi-party system is the _______
solution: One argument against a multi-party system is the high cost of conducting election. It is very expensive in both men and materials.

question: The highest grade in the Civil Service is known as the ............
solution:
The Administrative cadre comprises the most senior civil servants who organize and coordinate the activities of the ministries. They execute high-level administrative work, advise ministers in the formulation and implementation of policies and serve as the communication link between the minister and other civil servants.

Recruitment to this class is based on university education and very competitive written examinations and interviews conducted by the civil service commission. In this class are directors-general, other directors, deputy directors, assistant directors, etc.

question: The type of government operated in Nigeria between October 1st 1979 to December 3 1983 is called a __________
solution: During this period, Nigeria operated under the Second Republic, which was modelled after the United States' presidential system. In this system, the President is both the head of state and the head of government, separate from the legislature. This period marked a shift from the parliamentary system used during the First Republic.

question: A system of government in which a single person or a group who governs has unlimited power is a
solution: Autocracy a system of government by one person with absolute power.

question: The highest body which performed legislative functions under Babangida Regime was
solution:
The Supreme Military Council, SMC was the top highest organ of the Federal Government of Nigeria in the military regimes of Johnson Aguiyi-Ironsi, General Yakubu Gowon, Murtala Mohammed, General Obasanjo and Major General Muhammadu Buhari.
The body was instituted in 1966 by the administration of Aguiyi Ironsi deriving power from statute 8 of decree 1, it was later replaced as the highest body in a military regime by President Ibrahim Babangida in 1985 who created the Armed Forces Ruling Council.

question: The basic unit of every political organization in the Igbo pre-colonial era was the
solution: The Family Group is one of the most recognized institutions in pre-colonial Igbo society as the basic unit of every political institution.

question: The absence of an independent judiciary is a feature of
solution: In an oligarchy, power is concentrated in the hands of a few, and there is often a lack of an independent judiciary to check the power of the ruling group.

question: A recurring principal objective of Nigerian foreign policy is
solution:
A recurring principal objective of Nigeria's foreign policy has been the promotion of world peace with justice, especially through African unity, regional stability, and global cooperation. Nigeria's foreign policy is centered on:

Respect for international law and justice
Commitment to global peace and security
Promotion of African unity and economic development
Non-alignment and peaceful resolution of conflicts

question: In a parliamentary system of government, the executive branch is

option.a: under the control of the judiciary

solution: In a parliamentary system of government, the executive branch is combined with the legislative branch. This is because the executive, which includes the Prime Minister and the cabinet, is drawn from the members of the legislature. This is different from a presidential system, where the executive and legislative branches are separate.

question: Which African country successfully resisted European imperialism and maintained its independence?
solution: Ethiopia is the African country that successfully resisted European imperialism and maintained its independence. Ethiopia was able to maintain its independence after defeating Italy at the Battle of Adwa in 1896.

question: Nigeria is a founding member of which regional organization?
solution: Nigeria is a founding member of the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS). ECOWAS is a regional political and economic union of fifteen countries located in West Africa. It was established on May 28, 1975, and Nigeria is one of the founding members.

question: The current constitution of Nigeria is based on the model of which country?
solution: The current constitution of Nigeria is based on the model of the United States. This is evident in the structure of the government, which includes a separation of powers among three branches: the executive, the legislative, and the judicial. This model is similar to the one used in the United States.

question: A major event in Nigerian history that led to the suspension of Nigeria's constitution and the declaration of a state of emergency by the colonial authorities in 1953 was the ________.
solution: The Kano riots were a series of violent clashes in Northern Nigeria in 1953. The riots were sparked by political and ethnic tensions over proposed reforms in the Northern House of Assembly. The British colonial authorities suspended Nigeria's constitution and declared a state of emergency to restore order, leading to the dissolution of democratic regional governments. The Kano riots highlighted the challenges Nigeria faced during the transition from colonial rule to self-governance.

question: Which of the following countries operate a direct democracy?
solution: Switzerland operates a direct democracy. In a direct democracy, citizens have the right to vote on every issue that affects them, rather than their elected representatives making those decisions. This is different from a representative democracy, where citizens elect representatives who make decisions on their behalf, which is the system used in the United Kingdom, United States, and Russia.

question: The electoral system used in Nigeria is primarily based on __________
solution: The electoral system used in Nigeria is primarily based on the first-past-the-post system. This means that the candidate with the most votes in an election wins, regardless of whether they have a majority of the total votes. This system is used in Nigeria's presidential, gubernatorial, and legislative elections.

question: The process of selecting candidates to represent a political party in elections is known as
solution: The process of selecting candidates to represent a political party in elections is known as primaries. In the primary election, party members or delegates vote for their preferred candidate from a list of candidates. The winner of the primary election becomes the party's candidate in the general election.

question: The judiciary checks the other arms of government through
solution: The judiciary checks the other arms of government through judicial review. Judicial review is a process under which executive and legislative actions are subject to review by the judiciary. This is a key aspect of the separation of powers.

question: The commander of the army under the Hausa/ Fulani pre-colonial administration was
solution: The madawaki was the commander of the army in Pre-colonial Hausa/ Fulani Empire.

question: A naturalised citizen can lose his citizenship if found guilty of
solution: Treason is the crime of betraying one's own country. A naturalised citizen found guilty of treason will lose his/ her citizenship.

question: A form of government where the powers of the state is determined by the citizens is
solution: Democracy is a form of government where the power to select representatives rest in the hands of the citizens.

question: The Nigerian Civil War was fought during the military regime of
solution: The Nigerian Civil War took place between 1967-70 under the regime of General Yakubu Gowon (1966-75). It was a war between Eastern Nigeria (Biafra) and Nigeria.

question: The fundamental objective guiding a nation's foreign policy is
solution: Foreign policy refers to the decisions and actions taken by a state to pursue its interests within the global system. This means that foreign policy is based, fundamentally, on the country's own (national) interest. It can also be based on other country's interest only if they align with the country's own interest.

question: The Commission that examines complaints of civil wrongs committed by government employees is
solution: The Public Complaints Commission is a body responsible for receiving an responding to complaints from the public about the wrongdoings of civil servants and other government employees. They have the power to offer recommendations for the punishments of such employees.

question: The organs of ECOWAS do NOT include the
solution: The ECOWAS Secretariat is located in Abuja, Nigeria.

question: Price stability in the international oil market is the major objective of the
solution: The major objective of the Organisation of Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) is to ensure price stability in the international oil market. This is to ensure that fluctuation is eliminated and that every member-state can benefit from oil sales.

question: Which of the following is true of a parliamentary system of government?
solution:
Adherence to majority rule is true of a parliamentary system of government.

This is so due to the fact that the party having a majority in the legislature controls both the executive and the legislature from where the Prime Minister and ministers of his cabinet are chosen. The stability of this system also depends upon the ruling party controlling a reasonable majority in the parliament.

question: An important function of a constitution is that it

option.a: provide a framework for the study of government
option.b: facilities cross-fertilization of ideas of governance
option.c: serves as the fountain head of authority for the exercise of power
option.d: promotes citizen participation in government and administration.

solution: An important function of a constitution is that it serves as the fountain head of authority for the exercise of power, Constitutions prescribe how the power of the sate is to be allocated between the various organs of government. Most constitutions define the limits of a government's authority thereby establishing the various rights of a state's citizens.

question: when a bill passed by the legislature is vetoed by the executive, the action underscores the principle
solution:
When a bill passed by the legislature is vetoed by the executive the action underscores the principle of checks and balances.

The doctrine or principle of checks and balances advocates the use of one organ of government to check the activities of the other organs. When the executive vetoes the bill passed by the legislature, and the executive has checked the activities of the legislature, thereby putting the principles of the checks and balances into practice.

question: In the legislative process, a bill is a
solution: A bill is a formal proposal for new legislation or an amendment to existing legislation. It is introduced and presented for consideration by a legislative body, such as a parliament or congress, with the goal of becoming law if approved through the legislative process.

question: When Nigeria achieved independence in 1960, the head of state was the
solution:
When Nigeria achieved independence in 1960, the Head of state was the Governor-General, representing the queen of England.
One of the main provisions of the constitution of 1960 was that the governor general was made the constitutional ceremonial Head of state and a representative of the Queen of England.

This means the queen was the head of state but appointed a representative in the person of the governor-general to stand in for her, in her absence.

question: The most remarkable legacy of the 1976 Local Government Reform in Nigeria was the introduction of
solution:
The most remarkable legacy of the 1976 Local Government Reform in Nigeria was the introduction of uniformity in structure and functions.

In February 1976 Federal Military Government issued a degree establishing a uniform Local Government Structure throughout the country. The measure is significant in a away in the sense that the first time in the history of Nigeria a national rather than Region/state approach was adopted toward Local Government.

question: South-South cooperation is a major policy plan on which Nigeria bases her relations with
solution: South-South cooperation refers to the collaboration between countries in the Global South, primarily focused on mutual development, technical exchange and sharing of resources and knowledge to address common challenges. Nigeria, as a developing country, engages in South-South cooperation with other developing nations, rather than with developed countries.

question: Delegated legislature becomes unavoidable when

option.d: legislators have to proceed on a recess.

solution: Delegated legislation is used when the issues at hand are technical in nature. In such cases, the legislature delegates the power to make detailed and specialized rules to the executive or relevant agencies. This delegation helps address complex or technical matters more efficiently than the full legislative process would allow.

question: One major disadvantage of public opinion is that

option.a: the critics of government policies are always harassed
option.b: a vocal minority claims to represent the majority

solution:
Public Opinion is the beliefs, values and attitudes which are commonly held and expressed by majority of the people on a given public issue.

In some cases, what we regard as public opinion are the views of infinitesimal number of members of the public which means it does not represent the opinion of majority members of the public.

question: The structure of the civil service is based on
solution: The civil service is typically organized in a pyramid-like structure with clear levels of authority and a defined chain of command. This hierarchical organisation ensures effective management, control, and coordination of government operations.

question: Proportional representation is a system of allocating seats in the legislature based on
solution:
Proportional representation is a system of allocating seats in the legislature based on total votes in an election.

This system is used to secure representation in the legislation in such a way that each political party get seats in proportion to the percentage of votes cast in favour of a political party. The more the percentage of votes cast in favour of a political party, the more the more the seats will be allocated to it.

This system is used in multi-member constituencies

question: A major consequence of proportional representation is that it

option.a: reduces the chances of political instability
option.b: favours the development of a two-party system

solution: A major consequence of proportional representation is that it encourages the proliferation of parties, as the possibility of obtaining separate representation is there for each party no matter how small.

question: Centralization of power is the basic feature of
solution:
Centralization of power is the basic feature of a Unitary government.

Centralization is the process by which government power is located in a single central authority. The highest degree of centralization is realized where all powers of government are vested in a central authority. The degree of centralization is highest in a unitary state.

question: A major way of maintaining confidence in the electoral process is by ensuring that

option.b: elections are conducted in a free and fair atmosphere
option.d: elections are conducted as and when due

solution: Maintaining confidence in the electoral process primarily depends on ensuring that elections are free, fair, and transparent. This means that all participants have equal opportunities to participate, vote, and be represented, thereby fostering trust in the outcomes and integrity of the electoral system.

question: The fees collected by local governments at motor parks represents
solution: The fees collected by local governments at motor parks represents levies

question: The leading agent in the expansion of British influence in Northern Nigeria in the 1890s was the
solution:
The leading agent in the expansion of British influence in Northern Nigeria in the 1890s was the Royal Niger Company.

United African Company was formed in 1879 followed by the National African Company. This trading company helped the Emir of Nupe to deal with his rebels. In July 1886, a charter was granted to the company and the name was changed to the Royal Niger company with head quarters at Lokoja.

The Company also served the purpose of developing backward areas in the North as well.

question: One major factor that differentiates the presidential from the parliamentary system is
solution:
Separation of powers is one major factor that differentiates the presidential from the parliamentary system.

In a Presidential system, there is strict separation of powers as the President and the ministers are not members of Parliament while on the Parliamentary System, there is much fusion of powers. Here the Prime Minister and the Cabinet members are members of the Parliament.

question: The group of states that conceived the idea of the United Nations Organization included

option.a: the USSR, Italy, the UK and China
option.b: Italy, the UK, France and Japan
option.c: the USA, the USSR, the UK and China

solution:
The draft of the U.N.O was prepared under the auspices of the United Kingdom, United States of America, Soviet Union and China.

So the group of states that conceived the idea of the United Nations Organization included the U.S.A, the USSR. the UK and China.

question: Who among the following served as Secretary General of OPEC?
solution: Rilwanu Lukman, a Nigerian engineer and technocrat, served as the Secretary General of OPEC from 1995 to 2000. During his tenure, he played a crucial role in managing the organization’s policies and navigating the complexities of global oil markets. His leadership is often noted for its contributions to stabilizing oil production levels and addressing the challenges faced by member countries.

question: Nigeria's membership of the ECOWAS is informed by her desire to

option.a: develop a market in the sub-region

solution:
Nigeria's membership of the ECOWAS is informed by her desire to promote economic integration.

It should be noted that one of the aims and objectives of ECOWAS is the promotion of cooperation and development in all areas of economic activity, agriculture, natural resources, energy, transport commerce, monetary and financial matters, Social and cultural matters.

question: The independence of the Judiciary can be undermined through the

option.a: politicization of the appointment of judges
option.b: appointment of the minister of justice as the Attorney-General
option.c: confirmation of the appointment of judges by the legislature
option.d: payment of the salaries of judicial officers by the government

solution:
Independence of the judiciary has to do with the idea that courts should be separated from the Executive and Legislative arm of government. This independence is important to aid the judges to remain impartial to serve the constitution, act fairly, reasonably, free of any fear or favor. 

One way to ensure the independence of the judiciary is by not politicizing the appointments of judges. Judges should be non partisan and their appointments shouldn't be based off political affiliations.

question: A common feature of government is
solution:
A common feature of government is the making of public policy.

Government is made up of three arms the legislature, the executive and judiciary. The main function of the legislature is law making while that of the executive is formulation and implementation of policies

question: Constitutional disputes in states with written constitutions are resolved by
solution:
Constitutional disputes in a state with written constitution are resolved by the Judiciary.

One of the main functions of the judiciary is to interpret the laws of the land in courts.

It settles disputes between the executive and the legislature; between individuals and the state, etc.

The judiciary interprets and defends the constitution. It has also full power to declare any action unconstitutional if such action goes against the dictates of the constitution especially in a state with written constitution.

question: The citizenship of a country could be acquired through
solution: Citizenship in most countries is primarily acquired in two ways: by birth (either through the principle of jus soli—right of the soil—or jus sanguinis—right of blood) and through naturalization, which is the legal process whereby a foreign national becomes a citizen. These two methods form the standard framework for acquiring citizenship in many national legal systems, whereas options involving parliamentary legislation, registration and arbitration, or presidential proclamation are not typical means for conferring citizenship.

question: Socialism is a mode of production based on

option.a: state state ownership of the means of production
option.b: collective ownership of the means of production
option.c: national ownership of the means of productions
option.d: mixed ownership of means of production

solution: Socialism is primarily defined by the principle that the means of production—such as factories, resources, and infrastructure—are owned collectively by the community rather than by private individuals. This collective or communal ownership is intended to ensure that production and the benefits derived from it serve the common good, rather than generating profit for private owners. While many socialist states have implemented state ownership as a means of achieving collective control, the core concept remains the collective (or community) ownership of production resources.

question: The smooth operation of the civil service in Nigeria is mostly hampered by
solution: In Nigeria, one of the most commonly cited challenges affecting the smooth operation of the civil service is corruption and inefficiency. These issues have been widely documented as major impediments to effective public administration. Corruption undermines accountability and the proper use of public resources, while inefficiency leads to delays, poor service delivery, and a general breakdown in institutional performance. This combination often hinders the implementation of policies and the effective functioning of government agencies, which has significant repercussions on overall governance and development in the country.

question: The expenditure of public funds by the executive in Nigeria is controlled by the
solution:
The expenditure of public funds by the executive in Nigeria is controlled by the legislature.

One of the functions of the legislature is to approve how the government can raise and spend money. This is mostly done by approving the annual budget of the government.

The legislature in its job of checking the activities of other organs of government including the executive has the power to set up panels to investigate the activities of any department in government.

question: The most important challenge facing the Fourth Republic is

option.a: the need to develop Nigeria culturally
option.b: the successful conduct of the 2003 election
option.c: how to develop Nigeria's social institutions
option.d: how to deal with the military

solution:
The most important challenge facing the Fourth Republic is the successful conduct of the 2003 elections.

If certain things are not well done, such issues as rigging and election violence may plunge the nation into state of anarchy and this may eventually attract another military regime.

question: The activities of Nigeria in the international community are primarily influenced by
solution: Nigeria’s actions in international relations are driven by national interest, protecting its economy, security, and regional influence. For example, partnerships for trade, peacekeeping in Africa, and counterterrorism efforts all aim to benefit Nigeria’s stability and growth

question: Nigeria's relations with Britain was at a very low ebb under the
solution:
Nigeria's relations with Britain was at a very low ebb under the Buhari Regime.

The attempt to kidnap Alhaji Umaru Dikko and bring him back to Nigeria to answer charges brought against him was foiled by the British police. This strained the relationship between Nigeria and Britain in such a way that both countries recalled their ambassadors even though relations were normalized later.

question: Nigeria's high standing in the UN General Assembly is underscored by her
solution:
Nigeria's high standing in the UN General Assembly is underscored by the contributions to global peace.

Nigeria has been contributing much financially, morally, etc, to the liberation movements which is one of the main principles of the United Nations Organization. For instance, Michael Olisa et el (1991) pointed out that the prominent role which Nigeria played in the United Nations from the 1970s brought about its permanent representation in the UN. In addition, Leslie Harriman was appointed the chairman of the special committee against apartheid.

Nigeria has also played prominent and satisfactory roles in conflict resolution.

question: The one-time president of the United Nations General Assembly was
solution: The one-time president of the United Nations General Assembly was Joseph Garba. He was one of the top army officers in Nigeria.

question: The two-chambers of elected national representatives in Nigeria are called
solution: The two-chambers of elected national representatives in Nigeria are called the National Assembly. The chambers are House of Representatives and House of Senate.

question: The pre-colonial Yoruba political system as a whole can best be described as a
solution: The pre-colonial Yoruba political system as a whole can best be described as confederation of monarchs and chiefdoms

question: The Native Authority system was most effective and successful in
solution: The Native Authority system was most effective and successful in Northern Nigeria. That is why the question of indirect rule succeeded more in northern parts of Nigeria.

question: In a unitary system of government,power is concentrated at the center
solution: In a unitary system of government,power is concentrated at the center with residual functions. Some examples of residual lists are markets and chieftancy affairs.

question: One of the sources of a constitution is
solution: One of the sources of a constitution is common law, which consists of legal principles developed through judicial decisions and precedents over time. Many modern constitutions, including Nigeria’s, draw from common law traditions, especially in areas where statutes do not provide clear guidance.

question: In a democracy,sovereign authority is exercised by the
solution: In a democracy, sovereign authority is exercised by the electorate

question: The body responsible for running the personal affairs of senior local government staff in Nigeria is the
solution: The body responsible for running the personal affairs of senior local government staff in Nigeria is the Local Government Service Commission

question: The relationship between the tiers of government in Nigeria can be described as one of
solution:
Nigeria’s federal system constitutionally divides powers among three tiers—federal, state, and local governments—granting each autonomy in their respective spheres. For example:

The federal government handles defense, foreign affairs, and currency (Exclusive Legislative List).

States manage education, health, and agriculture (Residual List).

Concurrent areas (e.g., transportation) allow collaboration, though federal law prevails in conflicts.

question: One of the organizational weaknesses of the Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries is its inability to
solution:
One of the organizational weaknesses of the Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries is its inability to punish erring members.

Some member countries exceed the production quotas while some do not mind the fixed prices. Such defaulters always go unpunished.

question: The atomic bomb tests carried out around the Sahara Desert made Nigeria sever relations with
solution: The atomic bomb tests carried out around the Sahara Desert made Nigeria sever relations with France

question: A member of the Commonwealth that still recognizes the British monarch as its Head of State is
solution: Australia is the member of Commonwealth that still recognizes the British monarch as its Head of State.

question: A distinctive feature of the Richards Constitution was its provision for

option.a: unofficial members in the Legislative Council
option.b: a Legislative Council for the whole of Nigeria
option.d: official members in the Legislative Council

solution:
A distinctive feature of the Richards Constitution was its provision for a Legislative Council for the whole of Nigeria.

Northern and Southern Niger were brought together under one Legislative Council. That is, the same laws were made for the whole country by the same legislature.

question: The principle of checks and balances empowers the judiciary to

option.a: invalidate the actions of the other arms

solution:
The principle of checks and balances empowers the judiciary to invalidate the actions of the other arms.
For instance, the judges who can try government officials can as well declare a legislation ultra vires.

question: The pressure group that resorts to unconventional methods to achieve its objectives is called
solution:
Anomic group is the type of pressure group that resorts to unconventional method to achieve its objective.

It should be observed that Anomic groups are spontaneous, unorganized mob groups which are only interested in immediate achievements of goals or seizure of power. They use violence, arson demonstrations, strikes e.g students riot group riot, against hike in the price of fuel, etc.

question: The method used to determine the possible outcome of an electoral contest is
solution:
The method used to determine the possible outcome of an electoral contest is opinion poll.

Opinion poll is the questioning of a number of people chosen by chance to find out general opinion about something or someone. In civilized societies, newspaper columns contain public opinions on government activities.

question: On the basis of its structure,a political party can be classified as indirect if

option.a: its membership is acquired through other groups
option.c: it campaigns for votes through agents

solution: On the basis of its structure,a political party can be classified as indirect if its membership is acquired through other groups

question: The final stage in the process of enacting legislation is
solution:
The final stage in the process of enacting legislation is assent of the bill.

Third reading: In this final stage, a thorough look would be taken at the bill in order to correct certain errors connected with the drafting or amendment. A final vote is also taken on the bill at this stage. The bill now taken to the President for his assent after the bill had passed all the five stages is signed by the President.

question: The ultimate authority in a state is
solution: Sovereignty is the ultimate authority in a state. It should be noted that sovereignty is absolute power of the state to exercise supreme legal authority over its own affairs within its territory without any form of external control.

question: Under a parliamentary system of government, the cabinet holds office at the pleasure of the
solution:
Under a parliamentary system of government,the cabinet holds office at the pleasure of the legislature.

Under the parliamentary system of government, the Prime Minister is the Head of government while the King, Queen or ceremonial President is the Head of State.

The Chairman or leader of the party that won the highest number of seats in the legislature becomes the Prime Minister. He, the Prime Minister forms the government according to the constitution.

The Prime Minister can only choose his ministers from his party men in the legislature. The executive can be removed from office by the legislature. This could happen if the legislature passes a vote of no confidence in the executive.

question: An example of promotional pressure group is the
solution: Promotional pressure groups are primarily focused on advocating for a specific cause or value system rather than representing economic or professional interests. The Civil Liberties Organisation is known for campaigning to promote human rights and civil liberties in Nigeria, making it a clear example of a promotional pressure group. By contrast, the Nigerian Medical Association and Manufacturers Association of Nigeria represent professional and economic interests respectively, while the Supreme Council for Islamic Affairs functions more as a religious advisory body.

question: An example of a country that operates a bicameral legislature is
solution:
Nigeria operates a bicameral legislature composed of two chambers:

Senate (Upper House): 109 members representing the 36 states and the Federal Capital Territory.

House of Representatives (Lower House): 360 members elected from constituencies nationwide.

question: An institution that transforms a myriad of demands into a more manageable proposal is the
solution: An institution that transforms a myriad of demands into a more manageable proposal is the Government.

question: In the process of implementing laws, the executive sometimes performs
solution:
In the process of implementing laws, the executive sometimes performs Judicial Functions.

1. The executive can grant prerogative of mercy to convicts, especially those that have overstayed or stayed reasonable part of their term in detention.

2. They executive also sets up judicial panels of inquiry outside the control of the ordinary courts of the land.

question: Adjudication of conflict between the federal and regional governments in the First Republic was reserved for
solution: Adjudication of conflict between the federal and regional governments in the First Republic was reserved for Supreme Court

question: Prior to 1966,the system of local government in western region was based on the three-tier structure of

option.a: native authority, division and district councils

solution: Prior to 1966, the system of local government in western region was based on the three-tier structure of Division, District and Local Councils. In Western Nigeria, there was the 1952 ordinance which established three tier system---divisional, district and local councils. Each tier was given responsibilities according to its resources, with the divisional councils shouldering the greatest responsibilities.

question: In orientation, early nationalism in Nigeria was essentially
solution: In the context of early nationalism in Nigeria, the orientation was generally reformist. Early nationalism in Nigeria was often a response to the socio-political and economic conditions imposed by colonial rule. The nationalist movements sought to address grievances and push for reforms in response to the challenges posed by colonialism rather than aiming for a revolutionary or radical overhaul.

question: Problems arising from population census in Nigeria are mainly caused by

option.d: the registeration of the underaged people

solution:
The primary issue undermining Nigeria’s population censuses is political manipulation by elites. Political and ethnic leaders often inflate or suppress population figures to secure advantages such as:

Resource Allocation: Federal revenue and infrastructure projects are distributed based on population size, incentivizing states to overreport numbers.

Electoral Advantage: Larger populations translate to more legislative seats and political influence, driving falsification.

Ethnic and Regional Rivalry: Competing groups manipulate data to assert dominance, fostering distrust in census outcomes.

question: The arm of government mainly responsible for the implementing of law in Nigeria is the
solution:
The executive arm of government in Nigeria is responsible for implementing and enforcing laws. This includes:

Administrative Action: Ministries, agencies, and public servants (e.g., the police, immigration, customs) execute policies and laws enacted by the legislature.

Law Enforcement: Agencies like the Nigeria Police Force operate under the executive to maintain public order and ensure compliance with laws.

question: The exclusive list is reserved for legislation by the
solution:
The exclusive list is reserved for legislation by the Federal Government. There are about forty three exclusive lists.

Concurrent powers are left for both federal and the state governments while residual lists are reserved for the states only.

question: Power refers to

option.a: ability to impose one's will upon others
option.c: duties political parties demand of thir registered memebers
option.d: duties an individual imposes on himself

solution: Power refers to the ability to impose one's will upon others. It could also be defined as the ability of a person or a group of persons to enforce his or their decisions on others by the use of sanctions punishment or penalties against disobedience. According to Max Weber, "Power is the capability to control the actions of others"

question: Democracy is a system of government that is

option.a: constituted by the will of the majority of the citizens
option.b: imposed on the majority of the citizens
option.d: put in place to favour the party caucus

solution:
Democracy is a system of government that is constituted by the will of the majority of the citizens.

It could also be defined as government in accordance with the principles of popular sovereignty, political equality, popular consultation and majority rule. One of the characteristics of democracy is that all government actions must be open to criticisms and must be according to the decisions, wishes and aspirations of majority of the people.

question: The consciousness of a common identity of a people is called
solution: The Consciousness of a common identity of a people is called nationalism. Nationalism is then defined as the strong emotional awareness of belonging to a nation/state which leads to the struggle against foreign rule of domination.

question: Nigeria teamed up with other developing countries to assert a neutral posture under the platform of
solution:
Nigeria teamed up with other developing countries to assert a neutral posture under the platform of Non-aligned Movement. Non-alignment is the idea or notion of a group of nations in Asia, Africa, Latin America, Caribbean and Europe to be neutral in the cold war between the Western Capitalist and it Eastern Socialist powers.

It should be noted that many African countries have found it difficult to implement the principles of Non-alignment as required. This is mostly due to internal political problems which force them to look for aids and military assistance from either of the blocs. For instance Nigeria and Russia during Nigeria and Biafra war which in most cases referred to as Nigeria civil war.

question: Which of these is not the supreme organ of the OAU?

option.c: The Assembly of Heads of States and Government

solution:
The Organization of African Unity (OAU) had several principal organs, including:​

The Assembly of Heads of State and Government: The supreme decision-making body of the OAU.​

The Council of Ministers: Composed of foreign ministers or designated representatives from member states, responsible for preparing conferences and implementing decisions of the Assembly.​

The General Secretariat: Headed by the Administrative Secretary-General, responsible for coordinating the activities of the OAU.

question: The dominant idea behind the establishment of the Organization of African Unity is

option.b: to show the world that Africa can also do what Latin American and Asian countries have done
option.c: to provide a framework and opportunities for co-operation on common African problems
option.d: to promote economic development of Africa

solution: It is a forum to unite Africa and eradicate colonialism and imperialism.

question: A typical form of delegated legislation is
solution: A by- law in place, an individual or populace has stood in capacity of delegated legislation.

question: A referendum is a device to ensure that

option.b: legislators vote to resolve contentious issues
option.c: decisions are effected with the consent of citizens
option.d: bye-elections are held to fill vacant positions

solution: A referendum is a direct voting process in which the electorate is invited to vote on a particular proposal and decide on issues such as constitutional amendments, changes in laws, or specific policies. This process ensures that decisions reflect the will of the citizens, as it requires their direct approval or disapproval. Unlike regular elections, which select representatives, referendums allow citizens to have a say on specific legislative or policy issues directly.
